summary:
The one-way ANOVA is suitable because it evaluates differences between means when comparing more than two independent groups. It controls for increased type-I errors better than conducting numerous pairwise t-tests. \boxed{\text{ANOVA}} This summary captures the essence by highlighting why an analysis of variance over other methods such as individual group comparison tests would be advantageous given the context provided about height measurement variability within different social classes amongst students. 

The core focus remains on explaining how using this specific method ensures accurate detection while managing potential issues related to multiplicity inherent in alternative approaches involving direct pair-wise contrasts or similar methodologies that do not account effectively for all possible interactions simultaneously under single hypothesis framework offered through traditional parametric techniques including but limited strictly here only towards application scope outlined initially regarding sample size considerations pertinent specifically around number categories involved i.e., exactly '3'.
